title: Compare fractions with the same numerator or denominator
description: Compare two fractions that have either the same numerator or denominator.
topic_path: Compare fractions with the same numerator or denominator

hints:
Both fractions have a denominator of $3$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $3$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{3}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{4}{3}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD4$ thirds has a greater shaded area than $\purpleD2$ thirds.
$ \greenE{\dfrac{4}{3}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $3$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $3$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{3}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{3}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD3$ thirds has a greater shaded area than $\purpleD1$ third.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{3}{3}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{3}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $2$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $2$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{2}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{2}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]



Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD3$ halves has a greater shaded area than $\purpleD1$ half.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{3}{2}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{2}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $4$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $4$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{4}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{4}{4}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $4$ pieces per whole.
[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD4$ fourths has a greater shaded area than $\purpleD1$ fourth.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{4}{4}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $6$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $6$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{6}$. 

 [[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{5}{6}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{6}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD5$ sixths has a greater shaded area than $\purpleD3$ sixths.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{5}{6}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{6}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $8$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $8$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{8}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{10}{8}}$ means $10$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD{10}$ eighths has a greater shaded area than $\purpleD5$ eighths.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{10}{8}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $3$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $3$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{3}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{3}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{5}{3}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD{1}$ third has a smaller shaded area than $\purpleD5$ thirds.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{1}{3}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{5}{3}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $4$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $4$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{4}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.


[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD1$ fourth has a smaller shaded area than $\purpleD3$ fourths.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{1}{4}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{4}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $6$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $6$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{6}$. 

 ![Rectangle split in 6 equal parts, each labeled 1 sixth.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e84ff7718bbd1c3c16a92f74438c2e737194bf0b)

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{4}{6}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{7}{6}}$ means $7$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?
[[☃ image 1]]
The number $\greenD4$ sixths has a smaller shaded area than $\purpleD7$ sixths.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{4}{6}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{7}{6}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $8$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $8$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{8}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{7}{8}}$ means $7$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD{3}$ eighths has a smaller shaded area than $\purpleD7$ eighths.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{3}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{7}{8}} $

Both fractions have a denominator of $2$. We can show this by dividing each whole into $2$ equal-sized pieces of $\dfrac{1}{2}$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The numerator tells us how many of the total pieces to shade.

$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{2}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]

Which fraction shows the greatest shaded area?

[[☃ image 1]]

The number $\greenD{1}$ half has a smaller shaded area than $\purpleD2$ halves.
$ \greenD{\dfrac{1}{2}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{2}} $
Both fractions have a numerator of $4$. So, each whole will have $4$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{4}{3}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{4}{6}}$ means $4$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ third is larger than $1$ sixth, so $4$ thirds is larger than $4$ sixths.
$\greenE{\dfrac{4}{3}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{4}{6}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $3$. So, each whole will have $3$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{3}{4}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $3$ fourths is larger than $3$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{4}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $1$. So, each whole will have $1$ shaded piece.

$\greenE{\dfrac{1}{3}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ third is larger than $1$ fourth.
$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{3}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{4}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $2$. So, each whole will have $2$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{2}{2}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ half is larger than $1$ third, so $2$ halves is larger than $2$ thirds.
$\greenD{\dfrac{2}{2}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $5$. So, each whole will have $5$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{5}{4}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$ means $5$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ fourth is larger than $1$ eighth, so $5$ fourths is larger than $5$ eighths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{5}{4}} > \purpleD{\dfrac{5}{8}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $1$. So, each whole will have $1$ shaded piece.

$\greenE{\dfrac{1}{3}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$ means $1$ shaded piece out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ third is smaller than $1$ half.
$\greenD{\dfrac{1}{3}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{1}{2}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $6$. So, each whole will have $6$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{6}{8}}$ means $6$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\purpleD{\dfrac{6}{6}}$ means $6$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ sixth, so $6$ eighths is smaller than $6$ sixths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{6}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{6}{6}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $2$. So, each whole will have $2$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{2}{6}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $6$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$ means $2$ shaded pieces out of $3$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ sixth is smaller than $1$ third, so $2$ sixths is smaller than $2$ thirds.
$\greenD{\dfrac{2}{6}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{2}{3}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $3$. So, each whole will have $3$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{3}{8}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}}$ means $3$ shaded pieces out of $2$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ half, so $3$ eighths is smaller than $3$ halves.
$\greenD{\dfrac{3}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{3}{2}}$
Both fractions have a numerator of $8$. So, each whole will have $8$ shaded pieces.

$\greenE{\dfrac{8}{8}}$ means $8$ shaded pieces out of $8$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 1]]



$\purpleD{\dfrac{8}{4}}$ means $8$ shaded pieces out of $4$ pieces per whole.

[[☃ image 2]]
$1$ eighth is smaller than $1$ fourth, so $8$ eighths is smaller than $8$ fourths.
$\greenD{\dfrac{8}{8}} < \purpleD{\dfrac{8}{4}}$
